layer: Miss Lana / Home, Birthdays, School Shows, Characters, Pricing, show details
description: Integrate the owner's newly confirmed service details into existing pages while keeping the site light, emotional, and theatre-led. Add only the minimum information each page needs to sell its specific offer. Do not repeat venue logistics across pages; detailed operational information belongs in the later Planning Your Event route. Pricing remains only 'From $350'.
non_goals: Do not create the Planning Your Event route or full technical FAQ
Do not redesign page templates or add large technical tables
Do not add audience-size price tiers, fixed travel fees, discount promises, or package calculators
Do not finalize cancellation policies, supervision claims, media permissions, school documentation claims, or team biographies
Do not create new show titles or resolve outstanding repertoire naming questions
Do not implement form delivery, redirects, analytics, GBP, Search Console, or launch

decision_basis.origin: User-approved seven-task implementation plan after live-site review and owner questionnaire review on 2026-06-27.
investigation_findings: User explicitly wants marketing pages to remain light and visually similar to the current site
Home should sell the experience, not host setup, parking, weather, and policy details
Show cards/pages should focus on each story with only minimal service facts
Birthday answers provide a real differentiated offer through child participation and a celebration ritual
School answers support age-group sessions, consecutive performances, values/SEL language, and a recommended smaller-audience format
Characters and add-ons are confirmed but exact inventory and pricing remain unresolved

must_not_violate: Marketing pages must stay story-led, warm, and concise
Home and show pages must not become technical-company pages
Technical details must not be duplicated across every page
Birthday gifts, balloons, post-show time, and personalization must be worded with appropriate availability qualifiers unless canon marks them guaranteed
Do not make unverified maximum-capacity, safety, document, or permission claims

forbidden_execution_patterns: No new logistics wall on Home or show details
No guaranteed phrases for optional add-ons or character inventory
No generic corporate or engineering tone
No new page block unless the existing page cannot express the approved content cleanly
execution_guardrails: Prefer editing existing blocks over creating new blocks
Use progressive disclosure: brief page copy plus future Planning Your Event link slot
Keep Home practical information to a short format explanation and three or four proof points
Keep show-card metadata minimal and show-detail facts in one compact Show Details component
Use warm plain English and concrete experiences rather than repeated adjectives
If content requires an unresolved fact, omit it or mark the code/data slot as owner-gated rather than inventing text

readiness_notes: Shared facts and global copy must already be corrected

in_scope: Home existing format explainer and proof copy
Birthday page differentiation around the birthday child
School page concise operational suitability and values framing
Pricing page simplification to only From $350 and custom quote factors
Compact show-detail metadata and a reusable future link slot for event requirements
Small FAQ accordions on School and Birthday pages only where they answer page-specific buyer concerns

steps.title: Define per-page content budgets
actions: Document the approved role, maximum practical-detail level, and prohibited duplication for Home, show cards, show details, School Shows, Birthdays, Characters, Pricing, and Booking
Map each newly confirmed fact to exactly one primary page or to the future Planning Your Event page
steps.expectation: No fact is scattered across unrelated pages and technical material has a single future home

steps.title: Update Home without structural expansion
actions: Update the existing format explanation to communicate approximately 30 minutes of live story plus approximately 30 minutes of interactive games, dancing, and bubbles
Mention that scenery, props, sound, and typically three or four performers come with the production using one concise proof treatment
Do not add setup, parking, power, weather, deposit, or policy content
steps.expectation: Home explains the complete experience in a warm, quick-to-scan way

steps.title: Differentiate Birthday Parties
actions: Add or refine one compact block showing that the birthday child can be greeted by name and may join the story, dance, birthday song, and finale according to comfort and show suitability
Mention family/organizer-taken photos and optional birthday touches with accurate qualifiers
steps.expectation: Birthday is a distinct product, not merely the School page relabeled

steps.title: Strengthen School Shows
actions: Add concise values/SEL themes such as kindness, friendship, cooperation, and teamwork
Explain that consecutive performances can be arranged for separate age groups
Use careful language around a recommended smaller-audience format without presenting an unverified hard maximum
Keep documentation and procurement claims out until verified
steps.expectation: School buyers understand educational relevance and scheduling flexibility without a technical rider

steps.title: Simplify Characters and Pricing
actions: Present face painting/body art, standalone bubbles, and costumed character appearances as optional and availability-based
Keep Pricing to From $350, what influences a custom quote, and a booking CTA
Remove or avoid all audience-size tiers, calculators, fixed travel fees, and guaranteed discounts
steps.expectation: The offer is clear without prematurely locking commercial policy

steps.title: Keep show surfaces story-led
actions: Ensure show cards remain image, title, short premise, age/value metadata, and CTA
Use one compact Show Details block on show pages for approximate duration, format, and typical troupe size only where shared facts are appropriate
Add a non-functional or future-ready semantic link slot for Planning Your Event only if the target route will be created immediately by the next task; otherwise defer the link
steps.expectation: Show surfaces remain about the production, not operational requirements

success_criteria: Home remains visually and structurally light while accurately explaining the one-hour experience
Birthday page clearly explains the birthday child's special participation
School page explains values, age-group scheduling, and consecutive-performance capability without unsupported procurement claims
Characters/add-ons copy is accurate, optional, and inventory-neutral
Pricing displays only 'From $350' and custom quote wording
No setup, parking, weather, power, cancellation, or document details are repeated across marketing pages
Show cards and pages remain story-led with minimal reusable details

manual_checks: Compare page density before and after on mobile and desktop; no page should feel materially heavier or more technical
Confirm Home contains no setup, parking, weather, power, cancellation, or policy wall
Confirm Pricing contains no numeric prices except From $350
Confirm each show page still leads with story and imagery
Confirm birthday personalization is not worded as mandatory participation

closure_sequence: Step 1: Scope verification — confirm all steps, success_criteria, and deliverables are complete; confirm execution-safety compliance (no non_goals fixed, no forbidden_execution_patterns violated, execution_guardrails respected)
Step 2: Automated verification — run only the required_commands explicitly listed for the scoped task, confirm all pass
Step 3: Reviewer-grade pre-closure code review — analyze all changes against the review dimensions, record explicit findings with BLOCKER / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NOTE severity
Step 4: Closure resolution — resolve or explicitly defer findings with severity, write the final verification summary, and update the task report
Step 5: PROJECT_PROGRESS update — append the substantive change entry only after all previous closure steps pass

post_actions: Proceed to Planning Your Event implementation after closure
Do not add numeric pricing tiers in any follow-up without a new owner decision and canon sync
